_meta.description: Deterministic action recipes for the Guaardvark agent. These skip the see-think-act loop entirely.
_meta.format: Each recipe has trigger patterns (regex) and a sequence of steps. Steps may be hotkey/type/click for actions, or wait_until_settled/wait_until_visible for vision-gated waiting. Recipes can also declare a top-level success_proof â€” a vision-readable description that must be visible before the recipe is reported successful. Legacy `wait` steps still work but log a deprecation warning.
_meta.state: Minimal recipe set â€” restored from backup once the basics (click browser icon, navigate to URL) are reliable. Full prior set in recipes.backup-2026-05-11.json.

open_firefox.description: Open Firefox via its desktop icon

steps._comment: Vision-driven â€” servo finds the icon on the current frame

steps.target_description: Firefox browser window with URL bar visible

open_firefox.success_proof: Firefox browser window open with the URL address bar visible

navigate_url.description: Navigate to a domain-shaped URL in the current tab. Hostname must contain a dot OR port.

steps.target_description: URL address bar with text selected or empty and ready for typing

navigate_url.success_proof: browser address bar showing a loaded page (not a blank tab or error)

click_star_icon.description: Click the desktop star icon. One click, done. Fire-and-trust: no vision verify after the click, no fallback to the LLM loop. The servo's click success IS the task completion signal.
click_star_icon._design_note: No success_proof and no wait_until_visible step on purpose. Recipes without success_proof are terminal â€” line 2155 of agent_control_service.py only runs the verify block 'if all_steps_ok and proof:'. With proof absent, the recipe returns success cleanly after the click and the see-think-act loop is never invoked. This is the structural answer to 'click and stop' â€” model-agnostic, works regardless of what the vision pipeline can or can't see.

steps.target_description: the star icon on the desktop
steps._comment: Single click. Recipe terminates after this step â€” no follow-up verify, no fallback.

press_escape.description: Press Escape. Closes find bar, dismisses URL-bar focus, generally drops modal state. Plain time wait â€” wait_until_settled fails on pages with animation.

find_on_page.description: Open Firefox's find bar (Ctrl+F), type the term, press Enter, close. Plain time-based waits because YouTube's auto-playing sidebar previews never satisfy wait_until_settled.

scroll_to_youtube_comments.description: Scroll a YouTube watch page down enough to bring the 'Add a comment...' composer into the viewport. Pure PageDown â€” no vision dependency, no find_on_page brittleness. Escape first to drop URL-bar focus so the page body receives the keystrokes.

pause_youtube_video.description: Pause a playing YouTube video. Escape first to drop URL-bar focus (a fresh page load leaves keyboard focus in the address bar; an earlier attempt's Space went into the URL bar â€” delta=0.00 was the smoking gun), then 'k' which is YouTube's pause shortcut once the page body has focus.

focus_youtube_comment_field.description: Click YouTube's 'Add a comment...' placeholder below the video. Caller should have already run scroll_to_youtube_comments so the viewport contains the composer.

steps._comment: Vision-driven. The horizontal pill-shaped input reading 'Add a comment...' that sits BELOW the video title and ABOVE any existing comments. Not the avatar circle to its left and NOT any Reply input lower on the page.

steps.target_description: Cancel button next to Comment submit button
steps._comment: Distinctive verification â€” Cancel only appears when the composer expanded. If this fails the click missed the placeholder; recipe-level failure triggers the adaptive loop, which with the sharper target above should usually succeed on the second try.

focus_youtube_reply_field.description: Click the topmost visible 'Reply' button under a YouTube comment. Caller should have already run find_on_page <anchor> so the viewport sits on the parent comment.

steps._comment: Vision-driven. Small 'Reply' text button below the parent comment body.

like_youtube_video.description: [DISABLED 2026-05-16] Click the thumbs-up Like button below a YouTube video. Escape first to drop any input focus, Home to scroll back to the top so the Like button is in view. NOT currently wired into post_youtube_comment_via_servo: the vision-driven click consistently misses (qwen3-vl:2b miscalibration) and the post-click visual delta (outlineâ†’fill, +1 count) is too subtle for vision-side verification â€” false-positive matches the still-outlined icon as filled. Re-enable once the click is DOM-driven via the Firefox CDP debug port (port 9222). Recipe kept in place so it's ready to use once the DOM path lands.

steps.target_description: thumbs up icon left of dislike
steps._comment: Vision-driven. The thumbs-up icon inside the pill-shaped Like|Dislike control directly under the video title. Disambiguator: it sits IMMEDIATELY to the left of the matching thumbs-down icon. NOT the channel Subscribe button (purple pill, further left). NOT any like icon inside a comment lower on the page.

steps.target_description: filled thumbs up icon under video
steps._comment: Verification â€” once Liked, YouTube renders the thumbs-up icon FILLED (solid white/grey) instead of OUTLINED. If we never see the filled state the click missed the button. Recipe-level failure triggers the adaptive loop. Non-fatal at the caller level.

submit_youtube_comment.description: Submit the typed YouTube comment via Ctrl+Enter â€” YouTube's keyboard shortcut for the composer's submit button. More reliable than vision-clicking 'Comment' (which has misfired on the '0 Comments' header in the past).

submit_youtube_reply.description: Submit the typed YouTube reply via Ctrl+Enter â€” same shortcut as the top-level Comment submit, works on the reply composer too.
